metric_value: Kernochan argues for recasting Practice Summaries as hot-topics sales vehicles rather than primarily analyst training documents
notes: Internal methodology debate; reflects Aberdeen's dual research/sales function

metric_value: Most mid/large organizations have multi-tier hardware/software with multiple NOSes; key IS question is interoperability strategy not NOS selection
notes: Aberdeen NOS practice framing circa 1998-1999

metric_name: Microsoft NT Server enterprise growth trend
metric_value: NT Server's growing role in enterprise and relative decline of other NOS suppliers making IS decisions urgent and strategic
notes: Kernochan example text for Practice Summary executive summary format

metric_value: Recent enterprise adoption of Java development toolsets for e-commerce and distributed application deployment drives AD market
notes: Aberdeen AD practice finding

metric_value: Four major trends: Internet/intranet/extranet development; component production/assembly; distributed-app deployment; modeling/repositories
notes: Kernochan assessment of AD market forces driving toolset sales

metric_name: Component and internet development future impact
metric_value: Component development/assembly and Internet application deployment are two thorniest IT issues over next five years; will drive fully distributed multi-tiered environment
notes: Proved accurate; web application development became dominant paradigm

metric_value: NOSes proving more effective than ever as internet arrives; Internet requires directory/admin/security services that NOS is best positioned to provide
notes: Aberdeen finding; prescient — internet did strengthen NOS demand

metric_value: Novell is not a dead company; NetWare and IBM OS/2 Warp Server can play key roles in Internet Infrastructure via directory services and intranet workgroup support
notes: Aberdeen finding; partially correct — Novell survived but eventually declined

metric_name: NT Server overuse at enterprise scale
metric_value: Microsoft NT Server 4.0 is being overused at customer sites for enterprise-scale implementations it cannot support
notes: Aberdeen finding; prescient — Windows 2000 scalability problems were real

metric_value: Questions about which features will be included in NT 5.0 make it imperative for IS to scrutinize present implementations
notes: NT 5.0 (Windows 2000) released Feb 2000 after multiple delays; assessment accurate

metric_value: NOS suppliers answering user server-consolidation goals by delivering broader directory services and cross-platform administrative solutions
notes: Aberdeen finding on NOS supplier product response

metric_value: NT/Intel server market at inflection point with new Intel hardware and Windows NT 5.0 enhancements upcoming
notes: Kernochan example for Chapter Two summary

metric_value: Aberdeen expects conversion to IA-64 will take up to five years or more; allows greater NT/Intel server market segmentation between enterprise and cost-effective tiers
notes: IA-64 adoption was slower than even this conservative estimate; correct directionally

metric_value: Cost-effective NT/Intel servers will continue to plummet in price per transaction to less than $15
notes: Directionally correct; commodity server pricing continued downward trend

metric_name: Active Directory and NT clustering timeline
metric_value: Active Directory and advanced >2-server clustering expected to arrive in NT products before year 2000
notes: Active Directory released with Windows 2000 (Feb 2000); broadly on-time prediction

metric_name: NT Server viability gap vs Unix
metric_value: NT Server has significant issues to address vs Unix: interoperability; scalability; user counts; stability; storage management; security
notes: Aberdeen finding; these gaps were real and acknowledged by Microsoft

metric_value: Implementation and maintenance of NOSes has gotten so complex it often requires outside assistance; especially true for NT Server as enterprise NOS
notes: Cited as driver for professional services demand

metric_value: Microsoft mastered ISV recruitment and channel partner alignment; non-IS executive confusion of OS vs NOS helps NT Server adoption
notes: Kernochan observation on Microsoft's channel strategy driving NOS market wins

metric_value: All three major NOS vendors (Microsoft/Novell/IBM) showed sales increases over past two years despite NT Server dominance; market expanding overall
notes: Rising tide observation; NOS market growth concurrent with NT rapid growth

metric_value: Majority of NT Server NOS sales are in the workgroup segment rather than enterprise
notes: Aberdeen finding distinguishing workgroup vs enterprise NT deployment

metric_name: NT/Intel server market four major trends
metric_value: Four trends in 1998: boosting IA server capabilities; server consolidation; improving NT reliability/performance; clustering (work-in-progress)
notes: Kernochan Chapter Three market dynamics example

metric_value: Under Eric Schmidt leadership Novell plans products/services leveraging Java and Internet Infrastructure for installed base; must execute
notes: Aberdeen assessment of Novell's strategic response to NT Server threat; execution risk flagged

metric_value: NT 5.0 is the critical path product for Novell's installed base; Novell's task is to reassure customers NT 5.0 and its Internet Infrastructure will meet needs
notes: Kernochan's Novell supplier abstract example; correctly identifies Novell-Microsoft co-dependency

metric_value: Five-chapter master outline: Executive Summary / Summary of Findings / Practice Area Defined / Issues and Challenges / Supplier Abstracts
notes: Internal methodology document defining standard Aberdeen report architecture

metric_value: Aberdeen covers five Group Areas: Platforms / Services / Enterprise Business Applications / Network Services / Telecommunications and Networking
notes: Internal taxonomy; NOS practice situated within Network Services Group Area
